title: Section 26302
text: (a) Notwithstanding any other law, a foreign licensee may engage in commercial cannabis activity with a state licensee and a state licensee may engage in commercial cannabis activity with a foreign licensee, subject to the requirements and limitations set forth in this chapter. (b) A foreign licensee shall not engage in commercial cannabis activity within the boundaries of this state without a state license, or engage in commercial cannabis activity within a local jurisdiction without a license, permit, or other authorization issued by the local jurisdiction. (c) A foreign licensee shall be subject to the jurisdiction of this state for the purpose of actions taken for violation of California commercial cannabis laws and regulations. (Added by Stats. 2022, Ch. 396, Sec. 3. (SB 1326) Effective January 1, 2023.)
